name: Spinach in Phyllo
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Corn oil or soy margarine, melted
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. Bring a large pot of water to a boil. Add spinach and cook for 2 to 3 seconds.
3. Remove spinach with a slotted spoon and drain on a clean kitchen towel.
4. In a large skillet, sauté onions in olive oil until soft, about 6 to 8 minutes.
5. Add garlic and cook for an additional 2 to 3 minutes. Set aside to cool.
6. Combine cooled onions, garlic, spinach, and seasonings.
7. Brush one phyllo sheet with corn oil. Place another sheet on top and brush again.
8. Fold the phyllo into thirds vertically, creating a strip about 4 inches wide.
9. Spoon 2/3 cup of filling onto the bottom left corner of the phyllo strip.
10. Encase the spinach by folding the left-hand, bottom corner over the filling, forming a triangle with two equal sides.
11. Continue folding at right angles until you reach the end of the strip.
12. Repeat steps 7-11 with remaining phyllo sheets until all filling is used.
13. Bake for 12 to 15 minutes or until golden brown.
14. Serve immediately or store up to one week in the refrigerator or freezer.
Per serving: 214 calories; 6g protein; 168mg sodium; 18g carbohydrates; 14g fat; 0mg cholesterol; 84mg calcium
Vegetarian Gourmet, Autumn 1992/MM by DEEANNE
